mappings.rationaleAI: Addresses B (machines and machine components), C (machine tools), and the engineering principles behind mechanical systems.
Directly covers D.3 (robots) in computer-aided machining and D.4 (computer-integrated manufacturing).
Explains D.1 (computer numerical control), D.2 (CAD/CAM), and D.4 (computer-integrated manufacturing) from a computing perspective.
Examines the AI systems driving automated machining and adaptive manufacturing, relevant to D.4 (computer-integrated manufacturing).
Provides the algorithmic and programming foundations for D.1-D.4 (computer numerical control, CAD/CAM, computer-integrated manufacturing).
Explains materials properties relevant to C (machine tools for shaping metals and other materials) and B.4 (friction and lubricants).
Introduces nonconventional machining at the nanoscale, relevant to E (electron-beam, ion beam, and laser machining methods).
Traces the history of machine tools (C.1) and the pivotal transition from hand tools to power-driven machinery.
Documents the early history of hand tools (A.1), from Paleolithic stone implements through Neolithic and early metal tool development.
Explores design principles behind tools and machine components, relevant to D.2 (computer-aided design) and industrial design methodology.
